summary_sections.title: Key Concepts: Introduction to Nomenclature
summary_sections.content_html:
Core Concepts

  • Chemical Nomenclature: The systematic method of naming chemical compounds using IUPAC rules.
  • Ionic Compounds: Formed between metals and nonmetals; name the cation first, then the anion with -ide suffix.
  • Covalent Compounds: Formed between nonmetals; use Greek prefixes to indicate atom counts.
  • Binary Compounds: Contain exactly two different elements.
  • Stock System: Uses Roman numerals to indicate transition metal charges.
